cases.title: Co-operative values alignment vs. communal social orientation should stay apart
cases.task: Decide MERGE or KEEP for these two definitions.

evidence.definition_a: The extent to which the applicant articulates a genuine, intrinsic alignment with co-operative values — mutual aid, democratic governance, shared responsibility, community accountability — as distinct from purely instrumental motivations such as affordability or housing security.
evidence.definition_b: The degree to which the household frames its home life as embedded in shared, communal living — actively seeking neighbourly relationships, informal mutual care, and shared social life — versus preferring a private, self-contained household that happens to share a building.

cases.label_rationale: KEEP: the pair correlates at r=0.84 (a real confound — people drawn to co-ops often score high on both), but the definitions measure different things — values/ideology (A) vs. social behaviour (B). A household can hold strong co-op values yet prefer a private home life (high A, low B), or be intensely social without ideological commitment (low A, high B). Merging would collapse a distinction the committee may want to weigh separately. This tests whether the judge, from the DEFINITIONS ALONE (as production sees them), infers the diverging applicant and resists over-merging on the near-identical scoring.

provenance.note: Source run predates provenance capture (2026-07-16); models/prompt versions were not retained for the baseline Rank. Definitions are an exact, unmodified slice of the committed rank_baseline.json fixture.

cases.title: Co-operative disposition vs. community-building orientation should stay apart
cases.task: Decide MERGE or KEEP for these two definitions.

evidence.definition_a: The degree to which the household articulates genuine philosophical commitment to mutual aid, democratic governance, and shared responsibility as motivating their application — as opposed to treating co-op membership primarily as an instrumental housing vehicle.
evidence.definition_b: The household's active investment in cultivating social bonds with neighbours — hosting, organising events, welcoming newcomers, building genuine interpersonal relationships — as evidenced by current social habits and articulated aspirations, beyond minimum co-op compliance.

cases.label_rationale: KEEP: the pair correlates at r=0.858 but is a confound. A is philosophical motivation (why the applicant wants co-op living); B is active social behaviour (do they host, organise, cultivate neighbourly bonds). An ideologically committed but introverted applicant scores high on A and low on B. Philosophical motivation and active social investment are distinct axes; merging would hide whether fit rests on values or sociability. Tests judge resistance to over-merging correlated-but-distinct axes, reasoning from definitions alone.
cases.source: Rank run 4 (2026-07-16), consolidate_audit pair cooperative_disposition ~ community_building_orientation (KEEP); both definitions from run 4's dimension_report.

cases.title: Essay specificity vs. follow-through reliability should stay apart
cases.task: Decide MERGE or KEEP for these two definitions.

evidence.definition_a: The degree to which the household's essays cite specific past actions, named roles, concrete outcomes, and verifiable evidence rather than generic claims, vague platitudes, or corporate-filler language.
evidence.definition_b: Evidence — from prior roles rather than stated intention — that the household consistently completes tasks, closes loops, and does the unglamorous low-visibility work that sustains a volunteer-run organisation.

cases.label_rationale: KEEP: the pair correlates at r=0.887 but is a confound — writing quality (A) and behavioural reliability (B) are different axes. A reliable but inarticulate applicant scores high on B and low on A (and the inverse: a polished writer with no track record). Merging would conflate 'writes well' with 'does the work', a distinction that matters for a volunteer-run co-op. Tests that the judge does not merge on a high correlation when the definitions measure different kinds of thing (writing vs. conduct), reasoning from definitions alone.
cases.source: Rank run 4 (2026-07-16), consolidate_audit pair essay_specificity_and_concreteness ~ follow_through_reliability (KEEP; cross-run fork-heal — essay_specificity def from run 4, follow_through_reliability def recovered from run 1 where it was last a live dimension).

cases.title: Pet ownership and pet situation are the same axis and should merge
cases.task: Decide MERGE or KEEP for these two definitions.

evidence.definition_a: The number and type of pets in the household, which affects shared outdoor spaces, common areas, allergen exposure among neighbours, and the practical management of communal living.
evidence.definition_b: The nature and number of pets the household brings, relevant to shared-space use, neighbour comfort, and any co-op pet policies.

cases.label_rationale: MERGE: both definitions score the same applicant the same way for the same reason — pet count/type and its effect on communal living. There is no concrete applicant who lands genuinely high on one and low on the other (definition B's 'disclosure clarity'/policy nuance is too marginal to create a real divergence), which is the bar for keeping apart. At r=0.904 with subsuming definitions, a true duplicate. The clean, non-contested MERGE that balances the KEEP cases.
cases.source: Rank run 5 (2026-07-16), consolidate_audit pair pet_ownership <- pet_situation (a real MERGE). pet_ownership def from run 3 (the kept/older key); pet_situation def recovered from run 5's raw discovery report (worker 1), since the merge removed it from the settled report and the pre-fix audit row did not capture definition_drop.

cases.title: Licensed trade skills vs. hands-on trade skills — distinct, stay apart (contested)
cases.task: Decide MERGE or KEEP for these two definitions.

evidence.definition_a: The extent to which the household holds formal trade qualifications (electrical, plumbing, mechanical, masonry, arboriculture) that allow legally-regulated building-system repairs to be performed in-house without a contractor.
evidence.definition_b: The depth and breadth of licensed or practised physical-trade skills (electrical, plumbing, masonry, mechanics, arboriculture, upholstery, carpentry, etc.) the household can contribute to building maintenance and repair, reducing reliance on outside contractors.

cases.label_rationale: CONTESTED — both verdicts are defensible from the definitions alone, so this is review material, not an answer key. LEANING FLIPPED merge→keep 2026-07-16 (recorded, not silent): the human leaning was originally MERGE (the shared core is deployable building trades; the unlicensed-crafts extension seemed too marginal to matter for this pool). On reflection the leaning is now KEEP, on a definitions-first principle held INDEPENDENTLY of the judge — 'if two axes measure genuinely different things (formal certification for legally-regulated work vs. practical breadth), they stay apart; we don't reach in to merge distinct concepts just because they rarely diverge in one pool.' This is NOT the judge dictating the label: the judge returning keep (5/5 on 2026-07-16, and 4/1 before) merely turned out to AGREE with a reconsidered human view; the principle would hold if the judge said merge. The case stays CONTESTED because the MERGE reading remains genuinely coherent — at r=0.925 almost no applicant diverges, so treating them as one axis for THIS pool loses little, and that argument is real, not wrong. What that means: agreement is still not pass/fail here; consistency across repeated runs is the signal, and the judge is steady (5/5 keep) AND steadier than the production confirm, which flip-flopped keep→merge→merge across runs 5/6/7 on drifting definitions. (The score distribution that would settle materiality is deliberately withheld from both production and the judge — see ai-evals.md: r/divergence stay out of the confirm step.)
cases.source: Rank run 6 (2026-07-16), consolidate_audit pair licensed_trade_skills <- hands_on_trade_skills (a real MERGE). licensed_trade_skills def from runs 3/4 (stable, the kept/older key); hands_on_trade_skills def from run 5 (the live scored dimension whose vector fed the nomination in both runs 5 and 6). The SAME pair was KEPT in run 5 — see label_rationale on single-call instability.

cases.title: Three health/safety discovery dimensions are one concept and should fold together
cases.task: These definitions were each discovered as a separate dimension by parallel discovery workers, then folded into ONE settled axis by decomposition. Decide MERGE (they are one concept, the fold is correct) or KEEP (at least one is a genuinely distinct axis that should not have been folded in).

evidence.definition_1: The household's professional health or safety expertise that could benefit co-op members directly (e.g. first aid, CPR, occupational health).
evidence.definition_2: Whether the household brings health, medical, or first-aid skills that could enhance the safety and wellbeing infrastructure of the co-op community.
evidence.definition_3: Professional health or safety competencies — nursing, first-aid training, CPR instruction — that the household could deploy to improve building safety and member wellbeing.

cases.label_rationale: MERGE: all three definitions describe the same concept — professional health/safety competence (first-aid, CPR, nursing, occupational health) deployable for member wellbeing — differing only in wording and examples, not in what they measure. An applicant would score identically against all three, so they are one axis, not three; the decomposition fold is correct. This is the clean, non-contested MERGE flavor for the DECOMPOSITION pass (N discovery carvings of one concept), distinct from consolidation's pairwise post-score merges: decompose judges from definitions ALONE, pre-score, with no correlation signal at all — exactly what the model saw. Complements pet_situation_ownership_merge (a clean consolidation merge) with a clean decompose merge.
cases.source: Rank run 6 (2026-07-16), decompose_audit settled axis health_safety_skill (source_keys health_safety_skill, health_and_safety_skill, health_and_safety_skills; decision: 'All three keys are identical in definition and scoring — only the key formatting differs across reports. Straightforward merge.'). The three definitions are exact slices of run 6's raw discovery reports (fan_out_audit).

cases.title: Discretionary time availability and participation commitment are distinct and should stay separate
cases.task: These two definitions were discovered as separate dimensions by parallel discovery workers. Decomposition settled them as TWO distinct axes rather than folding them into one. Decide MERGE (they are really one concept and should have been folded together) or KEEP (they are genuinely distinct axes, correctly kept apart).

evidence.definition_a: The household's self-reported availability of spare time for co-op commitments, net of outside obligations such as touring schedules, intensive hobbies, or other regular commitments.
evidence.definition_b: How concretely and specifically the household commits to co-op work obligations — work-day attendance, committee seats, maintenance rotations — in their own words.

cases.label_rationale: KEEP: these are capacity vs. intent — genuinely distinct axes. A is how much spare time the household actually has; B is how concretely they commit to co-op work in their own words. The concrete divergence: a household can be genuinely committed yet honestly acknowledge thin hours (high B, low A), or make bold commitments with no honest accounting of constraints (high B, low A the other way), or have open weekends but say little specific (high A, low B). Capacity and stated intent do not move together, so folding them would hide whether a promising applicant actually has the time to deliver. This is the KEEP flavor for the DECOMPOSITION pass — the discipline of NOT over-folding when N carvings look adjacent but measure different things, the mirror of health_safety_decompose_merge (a correct fold). Judged from definitions alone, pre-score, as the decomposer saw them.
cases.source: Rank run 7 (2026-07-16), decompose_audit — settled axes discretionary_time_availability and participation_commitment_explicitness were kept distinct (decision on the former: 'Kept distinct from participation_commitment_explicitness (intent vs. capacity — a household can be genuinely committed but honestly acknowledge thin hours...)'). Representative definitions are exact slices of run 7's raw discovery reports (fan_out_audit); decompose settled entries do not store definitions, so they were reconstructed from source keys.
